intended_use: CoLumbo is an image post-processing and measurement software tool that provides quantitative spine measurements from previously-acquired DICOM lumbar spine Magnetic Resonance (MR) images for users' re
intended_use_full: CoLumbo is an image post-processing and measurement software tool that provides quantitative spine measurements from previously-acquired DICOM lumbar spine Magnetic Resonance (MR) images for users' review, analysis, and interpretation. It provides the following functionality to assist users in visualizing, measuring and documenting out-of-range measurements: Feature segmentation; Feature measurement; Threshold-based labeling of out-of-range measurement; and Export of measurement results to a written report for user's review, revise and approval.
target_condition: Lumbar spine pathology requiring quantitative measurement (e.g., disc herniation, spondylolisthesis, spinal stenosis, vertebral body compression)
ground_truth: Ground truth for segmentations established by per-pixel majority opinion of 3 independent U.S. radiologists using specialized pixel labeling tool. Ground truth measurements established by median of 3 radiologists' measurements using commercial software tool.
other_metrics: Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and 95% Confidence Intervals for measurements: Dural Sac Area 14.8 mm² (12.4-17.3); Lordotic Angle 2.6° (1.9-3.3°); Listhesis/AP Slip 0.9 mm (0.8-1.1); Mean Dice Coefficient (MDC) for segmentations: Disc/Vertebral Body 0.97 (0.96-0.97); Dural Sac 0.92 (0.92-0.93); Nerve Roots 0.75 (0.72-0.78); Vertebral Body (Sagittal) 0.95 (0.94-0.95). All primary and secondary endpoints met. Performance stratified by MRI manufacturer (Toshiba, Siemens, GE, Philips, Hitachi), race (Caucasian, Black/African American, Hispanic, American Indian, Other), gender (Male, Female), and age groups (18-21, 22-50, 51+).
